observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to:

Review all the transactions recorded under CIP account and prepare a CIPLC for each project that are still recorded under the account; and 

Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to recognize the completion of the four infrastructure projects, transfer the same to the appropriate accounts and record their corresponding depreciation expenses; 

Require the Municipal Engineer - Designate to:

Conduct regular monitoring of infrastructure projects of the LGU in order to prepare and submit a periodic status report of the same to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording and classification of projects in the books of accounts; 

Include in the Quarterly Reports on PPAs the implementation of projects undertaken by administration and furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy thereof to facilitate the identification and transfer of completed projects to the appropriate accounts; and 

Verify the status of the infrastructure projects implemented by administration relative to the construction materials that are still recorded under CIP account and provide the accomplishment and inspection reports to the Municipal Accountant and Audit Team for proper disposition; 

Instruct the Municipal Health Officer to provide an update to the DOH regarding the Communal Comfort Room Building and the completion of the additional civil works requirement to facilitate the release of the remaining project fund of ₱80,000.00; and 

Trace the unaccounted balance of CIP accounts totaling ₱14,356,144.63 by:

Reviewing and analyzing accounting journals and ledgers in CY 2016 and below to identify any misstatements in the said accounts due to errors and omissions and make necessary adjustments in the books, if warranted, for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and 

Exerting extra effort to locate the relevant records and documents thereof, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer - Designate, to facilitate the identification of projects implemented in prior years that were recognized in the books of accounts for proper reclassification/adjustments thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Closely monitor the submission of the Schedule of Unreleased Checks by the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the timely reversal of unreleased checks as of reporting date and prevent the recurrence of this deficiency; and
Make necessary adjustments in the books to recognize the unreleased checks totaling ₱475,972.54 in the financial statements as at December 31, 2024.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Record the proposed adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements; and
Ensure adherence to the recognition criteria for PPE accounts in the books.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Take up the transfer and receipt of the unexpended balances of the QRF and Mitigation Fund – MOOE of the LDRRMF in the General Fund and Trust Fund books when actual transfer of funds has been made for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and
Closely monitor the utilization of LDRRMF, in coordination with the MDRRMO – Designate and Municipal Budget Officer, to accurately determine the unutilized balances for transfer to the Special Trust Fund at year-end;
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balances in previous years totaling ₱13,064,468.93 maintained in the Trust Fund regular bank account to the newly-opened bank account designated for the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Record the proposed adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct amounts of the Subsidy from National Government, Food Supplies Expenses and Donations accounts in the Statement of Financial Performance for the year ended December 31, 2024, for faithful representation of transactions related to the utilization of the trust funds;
Recognize appropriate expense or asset accounts in the Trust Fund books upon incurrence of expenditures charged against the trust funds; and
Recognize revenue from the reduction of the liability in the books upon fulfillment of the conditions imposed in the receipt of trust funds.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Engineer - Designate, with the assistance of the MPDC, to:
Provide a status report on the implementation of aforementioned PPAs, including the reasons for non-implementation; and
Develop a clear implementation plan with a defined timeframe and regularly prepare monitoring reports to track progress;
Require the MPDC to prioritize the implementation of PPAs under Continuing Appropriations at the beginning of the year.  In cases where projects are discontinued, funds shall be re-appropriated to more critical and relevant PPAs; and
Instruct the Local Development Council to meticulously plan and evaluate the feasibility of each PPA included in the Annual and Investment Programs to ensure that these PPAs are procurement-and-implementation ready for efficient execution.

We recommended that Management:
Require the LSB to ensure that the SEF Budget is supported with PPMP and submit the same to the BAC for consolidation into the annual APP;
Direct the BAC to adopt the “Negotiated Procurement - Lease of Real Property and Venue” as mode of procurement for rental of real property and venue for official use and strictly observe the required procurement processes relative thereto; and
Discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to DepEd personnel for payments of related expenditures for activities funded out by the SEF, as they are not authorized disbursing officers of the Municipality.  Instead, payments should be made through checks in the name of suppliers, or in case payments through checks are impossible to make, payments should be made through cash advance of the duly designated disbursing officer, to strengthen internal controls, enhance fund accountability and ensure proper withholding of the corresponding taxes.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to conduct a thorough verification of the Class “A” Eligibility Documents listed in Annex “A” of the PhilGEPS Certificate of Registration and Membership to ensure that they are current and updated in order to uphold the veracity, authenticity and validity of the certificate and guarantee that contracts are awarded only to eligible bidders.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer – Designate, to deduct the excessive amount of ₱138,425.99 from the next progress billing of the contractor;
Instruct the Municipal Engineer – Designate to strictly adhere to the provisions and guidelines in the preparation of ABC taking into consideration those items not subject to OCM and Profit mark-ups, ensuring bid prices remain reasonable, acceptable and advantageous to the government; and
Require the TWG to thoroughly evaluate and verify the accuracy of the contractor’s detailed estimates during the bid evaluation and post-qualification stages to prevent excessive contract costs.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Cause the collection of the deficient business taxes totaling ₱140,556.20 and the corresponding penalties and interests from the concerned contractors; and
Collect business taxes from outside contractors who have undertaken projects within the Municipality applying the rules of situs of taxation.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to:
Initiate a request for a resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan enacted in the same manner as ordinances for appropriations described in generic terms, in coordination with concerned department heads; and
Review the LBP forms prepared by the department heads in accordance with the instructions provided in the Budget Operations Manual;
Direct the Department Heads of the implementing offices of special programs identified in the Annual and Supplemental Investment Program to:
Prepare and submit budget proposal to the Municipal Budget Officer before the local budget preparation for proper allocation of funds; and
Prepare LBP Form No. 2 identifying the applicable objects of expenditure for Special Purpose Appropriations, ensuring the correct account codes are used based on the Revised Chart of Accounts to accurately classify and incorporate each PPA into the Annual and Supplemental Budgets;
Instruct the Local Development Council to specifically identify the PPAs in the Annual and Supplemental Investment Programs and refrain from using generic descriptions so as to ensure alignment of expenses to the respective PPAs; and
Cause the refund of the misuse fund under the Clean and Green Program amounting to ₱99,530.00.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to discontinue the practice of implementing early cut-offs in reporting year-end collections to ensure timely remittance and deposit of collections, as well as accurate recording of transactions in the books of accounts; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the proposed adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements as at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
1.11. We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to discontinue the practice of
implementing early cut-offs in reporting year-end collections to
ensure timely remittance and deposit of collections, as well as
accurate recording of transactions in the books of accounts; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to restate the CY 2023 financial
statements to accurately reflect the balances of the affected
accounts as at December 31, 2023.

2.19. We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the
Municipal Treasurer, to diligently review records to identify
unreconciled items for reconciliation to ensure accurate balances in
the subsidiary ledgers of the Cash in Bank account;
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of
Unreleased Checks at every year-end to be submitted to the
Municipal Accountant and furnish a copy to the Audit Team in
order to facilitate the reversal of unreleased checks as of reporting
date; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to restate the CY 2023 financial
statements to accurately reflect the balances of the Cash in Bank –
LCCA and Accounts Payable accounts as at December 31, 2023.

3.22. We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Record the proposed adjusting journal entries to reflect the
correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial
statements as at year-end;
b. Review and analyze accounting journals in prior years to identify
any misstatements in the said accounts due to errors and
omissions and make necessary adjustments in the books, if
warranted, for fair presentation of accounts in the financial
statements; and
c. Observe proper accounting of collections for property taxes to
avoid the recurrence of noted deficiencies.

4.33. We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Require the refund of cash advances that have not been used for a
period two months;
b. Require all AOs to promptly liquidate their cash advances within
the prescribed period. Otherwise, consider imposing
administrative sanctions for unjustified delays in liquidation;
c. Conduct a review of unliquidated cash advances before approving
new cash advance requests for AOs with outstanding balances to
prevent overlapping advances;
d. Issue a demand letter to the former Municipal Accountant at his
last known address to facilitate the settlement of unliquidated cash
advances;
e. Regularly follow up with concerned government agencies to
ascertain the current addresses or assignments of individuals who
were granted cash advances while previously assigned in the LGU
and send appropriate demand letters for immediate liquidation of
their outstanding cash advances;
f. Cause the withholding of salaries of AOs who are still employed
with the agency until they have fully settled their unliquidated cash
advances;
g. Record the proposed adjusting journal entries to reflect the
accurate balances of the affected accounts in the financial
statements as at year-end; and
h. Record the utilization of cash advances only upon submission of
complete and valid documents, ensuring accurate and transparent
accounting practices.

5.25. We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Record the procurement of welfare goods, drugs and
medicines, as well as medical, dental and laboratory supplies
and semi-expendable properties to proper asset accounts
following the perpetual inventory method;
• Adopt the moving average method for costing inventories; and
• Record the necessary reclassifying journal entries for the noted
accounting errors for proper presentation of the accounts in
the financial statements;
b. Require the Supply Officer to:
• Coordinate regularly with the LDRRMO and MHO for the
maintenance of the stock cards for each inventory item in their
custody and to attach distribution list/s to the RIS;
• Conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies every semester
and prepare and submit RPCI to the Audit Team;
• Issue ICS to support the issuance of various semi-expendable
properties totaling ₱1,483,848.00 and submit the same to the
Audit Team;
• Provide the necessary details in the previously issued ICS and
succeeding issuances thereof, particularly, the serial numbers,
brand, model and useful life of semi-expendable properties
issued;
• Prepare and maintain the required forms, registries and
reports for semi-expendable properties and assign a separate
ICS control number for low-valued items and high-valued
items to strengthen the controls and safeguards over the semi-
expendable properties;
• Conduct annual physical count of semi-expendable properties
and to prepare the RPCSP;
• Require the former employees to return the properties
previously issued to them;
• Ensure that all outgoing officials/employees or retired LGU
employees return or surrender the government properties
issued to them;
• Discontinue the issuance of properties to Job Order workers;
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to maintain
complete Supply Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, for
each item of supplies and materials or inventory account and
conduct regular reconciliation of their inventory records.

6.14. We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly analyze the
report provided by the OIC – Municipal Assessor to trace the
breakdown of the Land account recorded in the books and make
necessary adjustments thereto;
b. Create an Appraisal Committee tasked with appraising the value of
the 61 parcels of land for which acquisition costs are not available
for proper valuation thereof and submit their report to the
Municipal Accountant as basis for recording in the books of
accounts;
c. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds to cover
expenses associated with land registration; and
d. Require the OIC - Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the
Property Custodian, to:
• Prioritize the titling of the 15 parcels of land with available
deeds of conveyance; and
• Gather all the required documents over the remaining 50
parcels of land covered by TDs aimed to secure TCTs in the
name of the Municipality.

7.29. We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
• Review all the transactions recorded under CIP account and
prepare a CIPLC for each project that are still recorded under
the account; and
• Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to recognize
the completion of the four infrastructure projects, transfer the
same to the appropriate accounts and record their
corresponding depreciation expenses;
b. Require the Municipal Engineer - Designate to:
• Conduct regular monitoring of infrastructure projects of the
LGU in order to prepare and submit a periodic status report of
the same to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording
and classification of projects in the books of accounts;
• Include in the Quarterly Reports on PPAs the implementation
of projects undertaken by administration and furnish the
Municipal Accountant a copy thereof to facilitate the
identification and transfer of completed projects to the
appropriate accounts; and
• Verify the status of the infrastructure projects implemented by
administration relative to the construction materials that are
still recorded under CIP account and provide the
accomplishment and inspection reports to the Municipal
Accountant and Audit Team for proper disposition;
c. Instruct the Municipal Health Officer to provide an update to the
DOH regarding the Communal Comfort Room Building and the
completion of the additional civil works requirement to facilitate
the release of the remaining project fund of ₱80,000.00; and
d. Trace the unaccounted balance of CIP accounts totaling
₱14,356,144.63 by:
• Reviewing and analyzing accounting journals and ledgers in
CY 2016 and below to identify any misstatements in the said
accounts due to errors and omissions and make necessary
adjustments in the books, if warranted, for fair presentation of
accounts in the financial statements; and
• Exerting extra effort to locate the relevant records and
documents thereof, in coordination with the Municipal
Engineer - Designate, to facilitate the identification of projects
implemented in prior years that were recognized in the books
of accounts for proper reclassification/adjustments thereof.

8.30. We recommended that Management take full opportunity on the
application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures for the one-
time cleansing of PPE accounts to come up with reliable PPE balances
that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

8.31. We also recommended that Management:
a. Require the Property Custodian to:
• Strictly comply with the required physical count of all
properties at least once a year using the proper format and to
submit a copy of the said reports to the Audit Team not later
than January 31 of each year;
• Maintain Property Cards for all PPE of the LGU;
• Provide inventory tags/sticker on all properties of the LGU to
facilitate a methodological system of inventory-taking and to
strengthen internal control on PPE;
• Review all the PARs of all properties of the LGU and cause the
renewal thereof when necessary, especially those that were
issued three years ago;
• Provide relevant details in the previously issued PARs and
succeeding issuances thereof, particularly, the serial numbers,
brand, model and property numbers of properties issued to
end-users;
• Require the former employee to return the laptop previously
issued to him; and
• Ensure that all outgoing officials or retired LGU employees are
required to return or surrender the government properties
issued to them before processing clearance;
b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to determine the estimated useful
lives of the properties with no depreciation cost based on the
experience of the agency with similar assets, and submit the same to
the Municipal Accountant;
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Update all Property Ledger Cards and perform reconciliation
with the records of the Property Custodian;
• Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to provide
depreciation costs for the PPE accounts totaling
₱205,728,233.28 upon receipt of the estimated useful lives from
the Municipal Engineer, otherwise use the useful lives provided
under DPWH Department Order No. 176 series of 2015 and
COA Circular No. 2003-007;
• Make the necessary reclassifying journal entries for the noted
accounting errors to reflect the correct balances of the affected
accounts in the financial statements;
• Review the lapsing schedule, identify all items with no specific
quantity and details that were lumped into one item, and
provide the corresponding depreciation; and
• Provide retrospective adjustment to all items recorded under
PPE account that fall below the ₱50,000.00 threshold.

9.9. We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Adhere to the accrual basis provided in recording transactions of
the Municipality, particularly in the recognition of accounts
payable and expenses; and
b. Record the necessary adjusting journal entries to accurately reflect
the amount of Drugs and Medicines Expenses account during the
year, as well as the balances of Accounts Payable and government
equity accounts as at December 31, 2022.

10.10. We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up the transfer and
receipt of the unexpended balance of the QRF and Mitigation Fund
– MOOE of the LDRRMF in the General Fund and Trust Fund
books when actual transfer of funds has been made for fair
presentation of accounts in the financial statements;
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to establish and maintain a
separate bank account specifically designated for the Special Trust
Fund for the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to prevent
misappropriation of funds for DRRM activities and facilitate
seamless reconciliation of the Cash in Bank balance with the Trust
Liabilities – DRRMF account balance.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared JEVs to record the proposed adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require all the accountable officers with outstanding cash advances and whose trips were cancelled to immediately refund the total amount; otherwise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the withholding of any money claims against the said accountable officers until they have fully settled their unliquidated cash advances;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the former municipal officials or employees of the LGU for the liquidation of their cash advances; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to send regular follow-up letters to the government agencies concerned for the cash advances granted to their personnel who were previously assigned in the LGU to determine the present address or assignments of the said individuals and send demand letters for the immediate liquidation of their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of reconciling records and the Municipal Accountant regularly follows up (verbally) the refund and timely liquidation of cash advances. Some actions (e.g., demand letters to former municipal accountant) have not been implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to strictly observe and monitor the procurement process of the LGU including the proper review of all Project Procurement Management Plans to avoid the practice of requiring advance deliveries.
observations.managementAction: The BAC strictly observed and monitored the procurement process of the LGU during the year. No advance deliveries were noted in CY 2023 transactions.

Minimal cases of reimbursement of the cost of repair and maintenance services were noted. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant now ensure that payments for completed repair and maintenance services are paid directly to the suppliers. Some pre- and post-repair documentation remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Submit to the Audit Team a Management Representation Letter (MRL) every March 31 indicating that the LGU designed, implemented and reviewed its internal controls and procedures over the use of e-payment systems; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Ensure that all online disbursements are supported with duly approved disbursement vouchers which would invariably bear the signature of proper officials;
• Prepare and maintain a separate disbursement journal to record all e-payments made by the LGU; and
• Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a daily Report of ADA Issued supported with the certified electronic list of successful and unsuccessful fund transfers to payees’ account provided by the LBP.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and maintained a disbursement journal for e-payments. The Municipal Treasurer is now preparing a daily Report of ADA Issued duly supported with the certified electronic list of successful and unsuccessful fund transfers to payees’ account provided by the LBP. The required MRL representations were not incorporated in the MRL submitted by the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Engineer and SWMO to fast track the determination of all the electrical requirements needed to support the operation of the waste processing machines and ensure to propose immediate project planning and funding thereof;
b. Direct the SWMO to propose temporary covers for the idle machineries and ensure regular maintenance/clean-up thereof; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the processing of the pending lease applications for final contract preparation.
observations.managementAction: Contracts of lease were already executed.

Management did not renew its contract with Shareable, Inc. in CY 2023 for the procurement of web hosting services. The Tourism Officer sent a communication request dated June 21, 2023 to DICT Region 1. Some updates and supporting documents were submitted to the Audit Team in order to justify the increased payments made in CY 2022. No IT-related plantilla position was created and some contract/training matters remain incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the MSWDO, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, to formulate specific guidelines on the grant of AICS and other financial assistance to constituents in line with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11 Series of 2019.
We also recommended that Management instruct the MSWDO to incorporate in the policies or guidelines the manner of maintaining records of AICS grants, referrals to other government units and even those disapproved in the assessment process.
observations.managementAction: Draft guidelines were already formulated by the MSWDO for review of Sangguniang Bayan. The manner of maintaining records and referrals were incorporated in the draft policies.
The missing cash tickets were already produced by the AOs. RCDs are now properly accomplished. Collectors are preparing their individual RAAFs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to reiterate her instructions to all collecting officers and deputized barangay treasurers to refrain from making alterations of the original entries in the duplicate and triplicate copies of ORs, and to cancel the official receipts and issue another receipts to avoid the deficiencies noted.
observations.managementAction: Minimal alterations due to errors were noted. The Municipal Treasurer reiterated instructions to collecting officers to avoid alterations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to review thoroughly the applications/programs they are using, especially those with automatic entries or dependent on formulas before printing them out; and instruct the designated liquidating officer to strictly validate the collections on a daily basis by reviewing not only the amounts, but also all the provided details.
observations.managementAction: Minimal incorrect data entries were observed. The designated liquidating officers now strictly validate daily collections.

The Management replied that they have been working on the reconciliation of their records in order to proceed with the procedures necessary for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts under COA Circular No. 2020-006.
Likewise, the Management agreed to start organizing and instituting proper recording system in compliance with the pertinent rules on PPE.
observations.reason: No action taken by the Management. Reiterated in Observation No. 1, page 25 of this report.

observations.managementAction: Management replied that instructions were already provided to all personnel concerned in order to trace back all transaction recorded under the CIP account so as to adjust the books accordingly.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office is yet to start tracing the transactions recorded under CIP account other than those noted in the audit observation, thus reiterated in Observation No. 3, page 32 of this report.

observations.managementAction: Management agreed to take appropriate actions to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 2, page 30 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is in the process of formulating strategies to comply with the audit recommendations since no available records are available to determine the debtor-agencies with outstanding obligations to the LGU.
observations.reason: No action taken by the Management. Reiterated in Observation No. 8, page 48 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to continue tracing the transactions recorded under the Land account and to reconcile with the records submitted by the Supply Officer and Municipal Assessor; and instruct the Supply Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to secure the documents required under the DENR AO No. 2016-21 to facilitate the titling of the land.
observations.managementAction: Communication was made to the DENR for the issuance of patent or title exclusively owned by the LGU. However, no action from DENR was available to facilitate titling.
observations.reason: No action taken by DENR; absence of action precluded Management from determining all necessary documents and actions to be undertaken (reiterated in Observation No. 1, page 25 of this report).

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous year’s recommendations that Management:

observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply and will adhere to the audit recommendations to prevent the further deterioration of the said unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: No action taken to fully reconcile records and prepare disposal documents; reiterated in Observation No. 25, page 91 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Agriculturist, to submit the documentary requirements for the approval of all the work suspensions and/or time extension orders including Resume Orders in the construction of Viga Diversion Dam, and the progress payments made;
b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to verify the work accomplished and any incurrence of material breach of obligations by the contractor in order to impose the appropriate penalties which may include requiring the contractor to pay liquidated damages, rescinding the contract and/or blacklisting the contractor from participating in any procurement activities; and
c. Require the Municipal Agriculturist to send a written communication to DA-RFO 1 regarding the approval of the time extensions of the project and the non-completion of the project within the period indicated in the approved Program of Works pursuant to the terms of the MOA requiring the LGU to make proper and regular coordination with the DA-RFO 1 in the implementation of the said project.
observations.managementAction:

Management verbally informed that they communicated the issues to DA-RFO 1 but the Audit Team could not ascertain the agreements between both agencies as documentation was not provided.
observations.reason: Some documents were submitted; other supporting documents and Resume Orders remain unsubmitted, thus the matter remains unresolved (reiterated in Observation No. 12, pages 51-55 of this report).

observations.managementAction: Based on contract reviews conducted for 2021 projects, the same deficiencies were noted. The BAC committed to take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Deficiencies persisted in 2021 procurements (use of outdated PBD, non-conforming bidder forms, incomplete post-qualification, missing CSHPs, lack of surety bond certification), thus reiterated in Observation No. 11, page 54 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC, through its Secretariat, to post the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed together with the approved contract in the procurement of infrastructure projects, goods and services in the PhilGEPS within the period prescribed under Sections 37.1.6, 37.4.2 and 54.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for transparency in government procurement.

observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 13, page 60 of this report; BAC committed to prepare PMRs and APCPI but had not yet submitted them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist, to submit the necessary documents to support the payment of honoraria to lecturers or resource persons pursuant to Section 5.7.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and DBM Circular No. 2007-1.
observations.managementAction: No documents were submitted to the Audit Team to support some honoraria payments. Management committed to secure and submit necessary supporting documents.
observations.reason: No action taken to provide required documentary support for certain honoraria payments (reiterated in Observation No. 25, pages 84-85 of this report).

observations.managementAction: Management stated they will look further into the deficiencies noted in audit and will take immediate action to address the audit recommendations. The Supply Officer and Municipal Agriculturist to coordinate with Municipal Accountant for reconciliation.
observations.reason: No comprehensive reconciliation or updating of WOABSLC/WOABSPC was completed; breeding stocks account remains non-moving and discrepancies persist (reiterated in Observation No. 23, page 85 of this report).

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require all the accountable officers to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances amounting to P54,666.00; otherwise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the withholding of any money claims against the said accountable officers until they have fully settled their unliquidated cash advances;

For the cash advances granted to personnel of other government agencies who were previously assigned in the LGU, require the Municipal Accountant to send written notices to the government agencies concerned in order to determine the present address or assignments of the said individuals and send demand letters for the immediate liquidation of the cash advances; and

observations.managementAction: The Accounting Department issued demand letters to the Philippine National Police to determine the current assignment of the accountable officers and another demand letter to the former DepEd employee. The incumbent Municipal Accountant said letters were sent and they will make continuous follow-ups to require them to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Some cash advances were liquidated (e.g., DepEd official). For others, follow-ups are ongoing; where persons are no longer in government service, coordination with agencies is being done (reiterated in Observation No. 10, page 52 of this report).

observations.managementAction: The LGU updated RFQ form and the BAC used revised procedures for some alternative procurements. The BAC Secretariat prepared Abstracts of Quotations and the BAC Chairman signed RFQs. Some missing documents, such as Mayor’s Permits for some suppliers, were still not submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Some improvements made (updated RFQ form, abstracts prepared); other documentary requirements remain missing (reiterated in Observation No. 12, page 57-58 of this report).

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required the submission of procurement plan as support for disbursements. Department heads were instructed on preparation of PPMPs. The BAC consolidated PPMPs into APPs where possible.
observations.reason: Some PPMPs and APP consolidation performed; deficiencies in preparation and submission persist for some procurements (reiterated in Observation No. 15, page 61-63 of this report).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to post the procurement data for all contracts awarded through emergency procurement under Bayanihan Act on the GPPB Online Portal including its APP covering all funds of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The BAC posted the procurements made by the LGU through emergency Bayanihan procedures.
observations.reason: Procurements under Bayanihan Act were posted as required.

observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, the BAC already requested the payment of their honoraria relative to the competitive biddings conducted in the same year. The unutilized proceeds from sale of bidding documents were transferred to General Fund under JEV No. 300-22-02-203 dated February 18, 2022.
observations.reason: Actions taken: honoraria charged in the same year; proceeds transferred to General Fund; adjustments recorded.

observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, the LGU provided some of the lacking Charge Sales Invoices and Collection Receipts to the Audit Team. The Audit Team noted that the SB still played a large part in donations and the LGU committed to secure necessary supporting documents and to have GSO administer releases.
observations.reason: Some documentation (Charge Sales Invoices/Collection Receipts) provided; other documentary requirements for donations remain lacking; SB involvement in distribution persists (reiterated in Observation No. 20, pages 71-75 of this report).

observations.managementAction: The LGU adopted a new trip ticket form and the Supply Officer used Requisition and Issue Slip; monthly billing statements were secured by user-departments which formed part of the disbursement vouchers. The Supply Officer was directed to inspect and request repair of defective fuel gauges.
observations.reason: Trip ticket form used by LGU was not the prescribed COA format; some required records (Daily Gas Issue Record, Monthly Fuel Consumption reports) still need strengthening; fuel gauge defects noted (reiterated in Observation No. 16, page 68 of this report).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from allowing the incurrence of advertising expenses for purposes other than the authorized items in RA No. 7160, the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, COA Circular No. 2013-004, COA Circular No. 2012-003 and other relevant laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, the LGU limited the incurrence of advertising expenses only for those that need public attention.
observations.reason: LGU restricted advertising expenditures to necessary public announcements.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that payments for overtime in CY 2021 complied with the specific rules on OT rendering, and departments were instructed to approve only necessary overtime. Compensatory time-off was availed by some personnel instead of OT pay.
observations.reason: No formal internal rules on overtime were established; controls and verification improved but internal policy formulation pending (reiterated in Observation No. 23, pages 79-82 of this report).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all department heads to submit copies of their monthly billing statements relative to the internet services availed by them. Likewise, require the BAC to coordinate with Globe Telecom, Inc., or its subsidiary, Innove Communications, Inc., the necessary documents or procedures in order to enroll the valid accounts being used by the LGU in one payment scheme requiring only the principal evidence of payment when settling bills and withholding the appropriate taxes therefrom.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, monthly billing statements were secured by the Accounting Office from user-departments and formed part of disbursement vouchers. LGU coordinated with provider for consolidation of accounts where feasible.
observations.reason: Billing statements now secured and filed; consolidated payment arrangements initiated where applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Co-Chairman of Local School Board to strictly observe the rules in the preparation of SEF annual budget pursuant to DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 dated January 19, 2017 for full disclosure and to facilitate efficient planning, approval and management of the fund. We also recommended that Management instruct the Co-Chairman of LSB to verify the unutilized balances from previous years’ appropriations and include the same in the preparation of current year’s SEF budget.
observations.managementAction: A supplemental budget was approved in CY 2021 to appropriate the unutilized balances of prior years’ SEF. The Municipal Accountant and LSB worked to align SEF budgeting with guidelines.
observations.reason: Supplemental appropriation adjusted some balances; however, SEF budgeting format and full documentation improvements remain necessary (reiterated in Observation No. 26, pages 85-87 of this report).

observations.managementAction: Management agreed to expedite preparation of the 10-year SWMP. A space for MRF and waste processing machines was provided but machines idle due to lack of electric connection; dumpsite remains controlled but pending sanitary landfill construction. Some PPEs and landfill budget provided; perimeter fencing and record-keeping to be addressed.
observations.reason: Draft SWMP not finalized/approved by DENR; MRF equipment delivered but not operational; security fencing and record-keeping need improvement (reiterated in Observation No. 21, page 80 of this report).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Treasury Office require the Municipal Treasurer to continue sending notices to delinquent taxpayers and making necessary information drive campaign on the awareness of payment in advance of the real property taxes in order to encourage the taxpayers for prompt payment; otherwise, apply the remedies provided under the law in order to generate more revenue that may be appropriated for the implementation of additional programs and projects for the benefit of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office conducted door-to-door delivery of Notice of Delinquency and yearly tax information drive/tax campaign and coordinated with barangay officials to assist in RPT collection.
observations.reason: Collection campaigns and coordination with barangays led to reduction in delinquent accounts (reiterated in Observation No. 29, pages 93-95 of this report).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer immediately transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balances of the 2020 QRF and MOOE - MF to the Trust Fund books for easy monitoring of the utilization and to ensure the availability of funds to support the programs and activities under LDRRM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant transferred the unexpended/unobligated balances of the 2020 QRF and MOOE - MF to the Trust Fund. The entry was made under JEV No. 100-21-02-011 dated February 24, 2021.
observations.reason: Unexpended balances were transferred to Trust Fund as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the suppliers to submit the Charge Sales Invoice for the said goods procured by the LGU in compliance with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 dated January 13, 2014. Likewise, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to release only the check payments for goods with proper evidence of payment.
observations.managementAction: The Charge Sales Invoices were submitted to the Audit Team and the Municipal Accountant adjusted the books accordingly. Collection Receipts previously used were supplemented with Charge Sales Invoices.
observations.reason: Charge Sales Invoices for the procured goods were provided and adjustments made; collection receipts supplemented (reiterated in Observation No. 31, pages 97-99 of this report).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Supply Officer to strictly comply with the required physical
count of all properties at least once a year using the proper format and to
submit a copy of the said reports to the Audit Team not later than
January 31 of every year;
b. Require the Supply Officer to provide inventory tags/sticker on all
properties of the LGU to facilitate a methodological system of inventory-
taking and to strengthen internal control on PPE;
c. Require the Supply Officer to maintain Property Cards for all PPE of the
LGU;
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to update all Property Ledger Cards
and have these reconciled with the records of the Supply Officer;
e. Instruct the Supply Officer to review all the PARs of all properties of the
LGU and cause the renewal thereof when necessary especially those that
were issued three years ago;
f. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to determine the estimated useful lives of
the properties with no depreciation cost based on the experience of the
agency with similar assets and the range provided under GAM for LGUs,
and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant; and
g. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal
entries to provide depreciation costs for the PPE accounts totaling
₱173,591,195.08.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to review the transactions recorded
under CIP account and prepare CIPLC for each project that are still
recorded under the account;
b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to verify the status of the infrastructure
projects implemented by administration relative to the construction
materials that are still recorded under CIP and provide the
accomplishment and inspection reports to the Municipal Accountant and
Audit Team for proper disposition;
c. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to prepare the program of works and
other related studies for the additional works required by the DOH in the
Communal Comfort Building;
d. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in budget preparation
the fund required to complete the Communal Comfort Building;
e. Require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the total contract amount
of ₱960,000.00 relative to the Construction of Communal Comfort
Building to Other Structures account and recognize the balance of
₱80,000.00 as Accounts Payable; and
f. Upon reconciliation and determination of all completed projects that are
still recorded under CIP, instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw the
necessary journal entries to adjust the CIP, Government Equity, and
other appropriate asset accounts; and the provision of depreciation costs
for the completed projects.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant
and Supply Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects, taking also into account the allocation
of the cost incurred for consolidated road projects to each road network
comprising thereof based on the Program of Works;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the recorded road
networks in the books of accounts with the inventory of local roads and
road maps;
c. Require the Supply Officer to prepare a Report of Local Road Network at
the end of each accounting period;
d. Create an inventory committee or group that will primarily handle the
inventory of local road network system;
e. Instruct the Head of the Inventory Committee to conduct annual
inventory of road networks using the Report on the Physical Count of
Local Road Network System; and
f. Upon reconciliation of records, require the Municipal Accountant to
adjust the balance of road networks in the books of accounts and
maintain subsidiary ledger for roads and road components for every road
network.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that Management:
a. Instruct the Supply Officer to secure the RIS or any equivalent proof that
the items were already issued or consumed, and submit the same to the
Municipal Accountant for proper adjustment in the books;
b. Provide a stockroom or space for the use of Supply Office to keep the
supplies and materials to be purchased including equipment which are to
be issued to the requisitioning head of department needing the same;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Card for
each specific type of Inventory;
d. Require the Supply Officer to record purchases and issuances of supplies
through the use of the prescribed Stock Card for each type of inventory;
e. Instruct the Supply Officer to consolidate weekly the RIS using the RSMI
and submit the original copies thereof to the Municipal Accountant;
f. Require the Municipal Accountant to record the inventories using the
perpetual inventory system and weighted average cost method, and
ensure that the relative expense will be recognized only upon the receipt
of RSMI from the Supply Officer; and
g. Instruct the Supply Officer to conduct a periodic physical count of its
inventories and report the same in the prescribed RPCI and cause its
submission to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to determine the nature of the
unreconciled subsidiary ledger balances of Cash in Bank account in order
to provide the necessary adjusting entry; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct further reconciliation of the
cashbook balances of Cash in Bank with the accounting records to
provide the necessary adjustment.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
review the composition of the Accounts Payable account to determine the claims
supported with complete and proper documentation, and to revert the invalid
claims to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
to reclassify the personnel services obligations to their appropriate liability
accounts, and to adjust the recorded liability pertaining to unliquidated cash
advances for travelling and training expenses, and other MOOE. The suggested
journal entries are as follows:
Amount (Ᵽ)
Account General Fund SEF
Debit Credit Debit Credit
Due to Officers and Employees 888,714.35 79,450.00
Leave Benefits Payable 794,641.74
Due to GSIS 152,576.27
Other MOOE 3,900.00
Travelling Expenses 25,266.00 1,500.00
Training Expenses 24,000.00
Accounts Payable 1,782,766.36 77,950.00

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the NGAs
whose checks for the refund of unexpended fund transfers became stale
on their end to return the said checks for replacement;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the following suggested
journal entry to adjust the checks which became stale while validating the
refunds made to the source agencies:
Cash in Bank Ᵽ 35,648.00
Due to NGAs Ᵽ 35,648.00
c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send written communications to the
source agencies confirming any remaining balances of unliquidated or
unexpended fund transfers;
d. Require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer, to
prepare the liquidation reports pertaining to the unliquidated fund
transfer;
e. Require the Municipal Treasurer to return the unexpended balance of
fund transfer to the source agencies or Bureau of Treasury, as applicable;
and
f. For fund transfers that could not be traced in the records or from the
source agencies, instruct the Municipal Accountant to request for write-
off in accordance with the procedures and requirements provided in
COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a summary report of
transactions comprising the said dormant receivable accounts upon
determination of their nature;
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer, after the reconciliation of records of
Accounting Office, to send written confirmation or demand to the debtors
concerned for immediate payment; and
c. In case of failure to collect said receivables upon exhaustion of all possible
remedies, the LGU may request the write-off of the dormant accounts,
through the Municipal Accountant, in accordance with the procedures
and documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require all the accountable officers to immediately liquidate their
outstanding cash advances amounting to P54,666.00; otherwise, instruct
the Municipal Accountant to cause the withholding of any money claims
against the said accountable officers until they have fully settled their
unliquidated cash advances;
b. For the cash advances granted to personnel of other government agencies
who were previously assigned in the LGU, require the Municipal
Accountant to send written notices to the government agencies concerned
in order to determine the present address or assignments of the said
individuals and send demand letters for the immediate liquidation of the
cash advances; and
c. In case the LGU failed to determine the whereabouts of the said personnel
upon exhaustion of all possible remedies, the LGU may request for write-
off, through the Municipal Accountant, in accordance with the
requirements and procedures of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to continue tracing the transactions
recorded under the Land account and to reconcile with the records
submitted by the Supply Officer and Municipal Assessor; and
b. Instruct the Supply Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor,
to secure the documents required under the DENR AO No. 2016-21 to
facilitate the titling of the land.

We recommended that Management:
1. Strictly comply with the required physical count of all properties at least
once a year using the proper format and to submit a copy of the Inventory
Report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 and every year
thereafter to ensure accuracy of the PPE account presented in the financial
statements;
2. Review all the Property Acknowledgement Receipts of all properties of the
LGU and cause the renewal thereof when necessary especially those that
were issued three years ago;
3. Provide inventory tags/sticker on all properties of the LGU to facilitate a
methodological system of inventory-taking and to strengthen internal control
on PPEs;
4. Require the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to maintain Property
Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and shall, henceforth,
conduct periodic reconciliation of the balances of both records; and
5. Require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal
Engineer, to prepare a schedule of estimated useful lives of public
infrastructure amounting to Ᵽ146,192,501.08 in order to provide
depreciation costs of public infrastructure account and submit the same to
the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management exhaust all possible means to document
the legality of ownership over the parcels of lots through Transfer Certificates of
Title (TCTs) in the name of the Municipal Government.

We also recommended that Management coordinate with the municipal
officials concerned in the documentation and processing of documents which are
vital in the determination of the proper valuation or appraised value of the donated
lots that were not recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended to Management the following courses of action:
1. Require the Supply Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer,
to prepare and maintain an Inventory of all Local Roads and a Road
Map. Likewise, the preparation of Report of Local Road Network and
Physical Count of Local Road Network must be observed yearly; and
2. Create an inventory committee or group that will primarily handle the
inventory of local road network system.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive provide a stockroom or space for the use of Supply Office to keep the
supplies and materials to be purchased including equipment which are to be issued
to the requisitioning head of department needing the same.

We also recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Verify the existence of the reported inventory balances and make
necessary adjusting entries duly supported with appropriate
documents such as working paper/schedule of issuances/releases to
user departments and proofs of releases and/or consumption.
2. Strictly apply the perpetual inventory method in recording inventory
purchases and outflow of the same pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS
for Local Government Units, Volume I. In addition, detailed
inventory records are maintained for each inventory item; and
3. Prepare JEV on the basis of SSMI and designate an accounting
personnel with sense of responsibility to maintain dutifully an
updated Supplies Ledger Cards for each specific type of Inventories.
b. Require the Supply Officer-Designate to:
1. Prioritize the conduct of a periodic physical count of its inventories
and report the same in the prescribed Report of Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) and cause its submission to the Auditor not later
than July 31 and January 31 of each year in accordance with Section
124 of NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
2. Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were
issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued. The
SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS shall be submitted to
the Chief Accountant; and
3. Maintain Stock Cards for each specific type of Inventories.

We recommended Management the following courses of actions:
1. Require the disposal of the unserviceable properties of the agency in
accordance with the requirements and procedures pursuant to Sections 76
and 79 of PD 1445 using the IIRUP form to generate additional funds,
when sold, to be used for the agency’s operations, prevent said
unserviceable properties from further deterioration and loss of value, and
allow the agency to use the space being occupied by these unserviceable
properties;
2. Require the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to expedite the
reconciliation of their respective records in order to determine the value
of all reported unserviceable properties and to provide detailed
breakdown of procured group of PPE recorded as one line item both in
the accounting and inventory records;
3. Secure the Invoice Receipt for Property from the BIR, BFAR and LMP
of the transferred motor vehicles of the Municipality or Clearance to
facilitate the disposal of the same; and
4. Require the Supply Officer to reconcile the differences noted between the
reported unserviceable properties in the year 2018 and 2019, and submit
the revised IIRUP to the Audit Team, if any.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to implement stricter control and monitoring of all accountable forms
issued to her and as entrusted by her to all collecting officers in order to report
accurately the amount of collections and remittances, and to avoid the possibility of
misappropriation of government funds.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer exert more effort in identifying the nature of
the unreconciled items pertaining to previous years and cause the immediate
adjustment of the valid reconciling items in order to present the correct balances of
the Cash in Bank account and other related accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
1. Require all municipal officials and employees concerned to immediately
liquidate the outstanding cash advances amounting to P122,149.40;
otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against them for any
unjustifiable reasons on their failure to liquidate cash advances within the
prescribed period, in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular
No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Civil Service Commission Memo
Circular No. 12 dated July 10, 2012;
2. Cause the withholding of any money claims against the municipal officials
and employees concerned until they have fully settled their unliquidated
cash advances;
3. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA
Circular No. 96-004 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash
advances relative to travels and trainings; and
4. Determine and exhaust all possible remedies for the liquidation of dormant
unliquidated cash advances; otherwise, the agency may request for write-off
in accordance with requirements and procedures of COA Circular No.
2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to collect the receivables from debtors. In case
of failure to collect said receivables upon exhaustion of all possible remedies, secure
pertinent documents to request for write-off to clear the dormant accounts in
accordance with the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended Management the following courses of action:
1. Instruct all department heads to submit their PPMPs for the
procurement of infrastructure projects, and other goods and services
including the commonly-used office supplies and equipment to the BAC
for consolidation into an APP;
2. Require the BAC to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 7.3.5, 7.4
and 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 by preparing and/or
submitting to GPPB the final APP and semestral PMR within the
stipulated deadline and using the prescribed format;
3. Require the BAC to invite observers in the conduct of Bid Evaluation to
comply fully with the principle of transparency being encouraged by RA
9184 or the Government Procurement Reform Act;
4. Instruct the BAC-TWG to observe strictly the guidelines in the conduct
and preparation of Bid Evaluation and Post-Qualification Reports to
establish properly the eligibility of the participating bidders, and require
the use of the format of contracts incorporated in the 5th edition of
Philippine Bidding Documents as endorsed by the GPPB for government
procurement;
5. Advise the BAC to post the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in the
PhilGEPS website within the period prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR
of RA 9184, and ensure that the required documents are complete and
proper before approving contracts and issuing Notice to Proceed; and
6. Require the immediate submission of the lacking documents to the audit
team for verification and audit purposes in accordance with Section 4 (6)
of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. We also
recommended the BAC to prioritize the submission of documents
pertaining to the completed and paid projects which are necessary for the
conduct of technical evaluation and inspection.

We recommended that Management require the suppliers/contractors to
issue Sales Invoice and/or Official Receipts on various procurement of goods made
in compliance with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 dated January 13,
2014 and likewise, strictly observe said regulations on future disbursements to be
made.

We recommended that Management exert more effort to ensure the
submission of all the perfected contracts together with the complete supporting
documents within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009 to enable the auditor to conduct timely review and evaluation of
the transactions.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Submit the lacking documents to support the distribution of the donated
goods/materials and to determine the purpose/objective of the recipients.
b. Require the barangay officials to submit pertinent reports documenting
the utilization of the supplies and materials received from the
Municipality such as, but not limited to Program of Work, pictures
before, during and after work completion, Inspection Report, Property
Acknowledgement Receipt and/or Inventory Custodian Slip;
c. Require the officials concerned to charge the donationsonly to the Office
of the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Social Welfare and Development
Office. The SB members should stop from granting donations/financial
assistance in cash or in kind to individuals or associations pursuant to
Section 447 of RA 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code
of 1991; and
d. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that
programs/projects/activities or items of appropriations in the annual
budget are specifically identified and not expressed in generic terms;
otherwise, secure authority from the Sangguniang Bayan before
implementing any project chargeable thereto.

We recommended Management the following courses of action:
1. Submit legal basis in the procurement of sacks of rice directly purchased
from the Supplier without adhering to the prescribed procurement
processes under the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184;
2. Submit proof or documentation that the sacks of rice purchased by the
Municipality were actually distributed/issued to the NGAs concerned,
and the SB Resolution authorizing the grant of sacks of rice to the NGAs
personnel and providing details thereof;
3. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that
programs/projects/activities or items of appropriations are identified in
the annual budget and are not expressed in generic terms before
submission to the Sanguniag Bayan (SB) for review and approval;
otherwise, secure authority from the SB to support the implementation of
any project, program or activity; and
4. Provide basis for granting rice subsidies to uniformed personnel of PNP
and DND in addition to the rice allowance from their respective agencies.

We recommended that Management:
1. Instruct all employees and officials concerned to strictly adhere to the
provisions of Executive Order No. 77 and other related laws, rules and
regulations for expenses on travel, seminars and conferences; and
2. Require the officials and employees concerned to submit the required
documents to support the claims on traveling and seminar expenses.

We recommended Management the following courses of action:
1. Require the drivers to properly accomplish the trip tickets indicating all
the necessary information for the control and monitoring of fuel
consumption, and instruct them to ensure that passengers will sign
thereon;
2. Direct the use of the Requisition and Issue Voucher form in all fuel
requisition and issuance transactions;
3. Instruct the gasoline station to indicate in the individual invoices, the
plate number of the vehicle requesting fuel;
4. Instruct the officials concerned to prepare Daily Gas Issue Record,
Summary of Requisition and Issue Voucher, Monthly Report of Official
Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; and
5. For vehicles and heavy equipment with defective fuel gauge, require the
Supply Officer to undertake immediately the repair thereof, and shall
henceforth, ensure to hold the drivers responsible for not reporting
defective fuel gauge.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and HRMO to submit all the required documents to support the
payments of terminal leave benefits in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001,
and to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that Management refrain from incurring advertising
expenses for purposes other than the authorized items in R.A. 7160, the 2016 Revised
IRR of R.A. 9184, COA Circular No. 2013-004, COA Circular No. 2012-003 and other
relevant laws, rules and regulations. Likewise, instruct the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant to observe complete documentation
and that the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 are faithfully adhered
to in the procurement of advertising services.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the employees
concerned to submit the necessary documents to support the payment of honoraria
to lecturers, resource persons and facilitators pursuant to Section 5.7.4 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 and DBM Circular No. 2007-1.
We also recommended that Management submit written explanation or
justification citing their and providing basis in granting honoraria to agency
officials/employees during the “Formulation of Executive Legislative / Capacity
Development Agenda”, otherwise, refund the relative amount of Ᵽ31,000.00 paid to
them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the HRMO to
maximize the use of the biometric system in the recording of employees’ daily
record of attendance to hasten the internal control thereto and institute a
comprehensive internal control system guidelines for its implementation including,
but not limited to the procedures to be undertaken if an employee failed to enter
his/her fingerprint in the system, system shutdowns and data security. Likewise, the
monthly record of attendance (DTR) to be submitted to the Audit Team shall be
supported with the log schedules generated from the biometric machine.

We recommended that the Accounting Office, in cooperation with the
Treasury Office, ensure the complete submission of accounts within the prescribed
period for timely verification and audit of accounts pursuant to Section 7.2.1 of the
2009 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts, and Section 39 (1)
and (3) of P.D 1445 to avoid disciplinary actions and/or penalties provided for under
Section 39 (3) of P.D 1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
1. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit monthly
Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances
(SAAOB) not later than the twentieth day after the end of each month;
2. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to
perform quarterly reconciliation of the budget and actual amounts in
order to prepare periodically the Statement of Comparison of Budget and
Actual Amounts (SCBAA); and
3. Submit the SAAOB and SBAA for the year ending December 31, 2019
and ensure that the year-end financial statements are submitted together
with all the required schedules and supporting documents on or before
the 14th day of February of the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountatnt to transfer all unexpended/unobligated
balances of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster Risk Reduction Management
Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities –
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020) in
the Trust Fund book.

We recommended that Management ensure the observance of the rules set
forth under COA Circular No. 2014-002 and other related laws, rules and regulations
on the accounting, inventory and monitoring guidelines for the control and
monitoring of purchased supplies and materials charged to the LDRRMF to ensure
that supplies and materials procured by the agency relative to disaster response are
accounted fully and properly.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to ensure
that the annual GAD Plan and Budget strictly follow the rules laid down under
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 specifically on
the presentation of attributed programs.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the amount
appropriated for Gender and Development (GAD) to afford full implementation of
the identified programs, activities, and projects (PAPs) in the GAD Plan so that the
benefits that could be derived therefrom could be availed by the intended
beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board, expedite the preparation of the 10-year Solid Waste
Management Plan in accordance with RA 9003 and other pertinent regulations
issued by the DENR and DILG to ensure the protection of the public from health
and environmental hazards.
Likewise, speed up the negotiation on solid waste management contract with
Municipality of Burgos to address the disposal of waste and stop the use of the
LGU’s existing controlled dumpsite.

We recommended that the Local School Board strictly observe the rules in
the preparation of annual SEF budget pursuant to DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint
Circular No. 1 dated January 19, 2017 for full disclosure and to facilitate efficient
planning, approval and management of the fund.

We recommended that Management require the Local School Board to
appropriate and utilize the Special Education Fund (SEF) in accordance with Section
4 of Dep-Ed-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 and stop the incurrence
of expenditures which are not related with the said Joint Circular. The Local Chief
Executive, as the head of the Local School Board, should strictly adhere to the
regulation in the prioritization and utilization of the SEF as required in RA 7160 and
the Joint Circular.
