observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Strictly comply with the required physical count of all properties at least once a year and to submit a copy of the Inventory Report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 and every year thereafter to ensure accuracy of the PPE account presented in the financial statements.
Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and shall, henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of the balances of both records.

We recommended that Management exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership over the parcels of lots through Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipal Government.
Likewise, we recommended that Management coordinate with municipal officials concerned in the documentation and processing of documents, which are vital in the determination of the proper valuation or appraised value of the donated lots that were not recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the proper recording of every financial transactions of the Municipality in conformity with PPSAS Chart of Accounts, and to prepare and effect the necessary adjusting entries to present fairly all accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer exert more effort in identifying the nature of the reconciling items pertaining to CY 2016 and cause the immediate adjustment of the valid reconciling items in order to present the correct balances of the Cash in Bank account and other related accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management send written notices to the payees of existing stale checks and require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to comply with the foregoing rules laid out under Section 59 of NGAS Manual for LGUs for proper disposition/adjustments of the stale checks in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive provide a stockroom or space for the use of Supply Office to keep the supplies and materials to be purchased including equipment which are to be issued to the requisitioning head of department needing the same.
We also recommended that Management:
Require the Supply Officer-Designate to:
Prioritize the conduct of a periodic physical count of its inventories and report the same in the prescribed Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and cause its submission to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year in accordance with Section 124 of NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued. The SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS shall be submitted to the Chief Accountant; and
Maintain Stock Cards for each specific type of Inventories.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Strictly apply the perpetual inventory method in recording inventory purchases and outflow of the same pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS for Local Government Units, Volume I. In addition, detailed inventory records are maintained for each inventory item;
Prepare JEV on the basis of SSMI and designate an accounting personnel with sense of responsibility to maintain dutifully an updated Supplies Ledger Cards for each specific type of Inventories; and
Verify the existence of the reported inventory balances and make necessary adjusting entries duly supported with appropriate documents such as working paper/schedule of issuances/releases to user departments and proofs of releases and/or consumption.

We recommended that Management dispose the unserviceable properties of the agency in accordance with Sections 76 and 79 of PD 1445 to generate additional funds, when sold, to be used for the agency’s operations, prevent said unserviceable properties from further deterioration and loss of value, and allow the agency to use the space being occupied by these unserviceable properties.
Likewise, Management should secure the Invoice Receipt for Property from the BIR, BFAR and LMP of the transferred motor vehicles of the Municipality or Clearance to facilitate the disposal of the same.

We recommended that Management exert more effort in securing pertinent documents to facilitate the request for write-off of the said dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, and to reconcile and clear the books of accounts of dormant receivables and fund transfers.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to instill to all LGU officials and employees the necessity of submitting complete and proper documentation for all claims pursuant to the provisions of Sections 1.1.4 and 1.2.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.
Likewise, require the officials and employees concerned to submit the required travel orders, paper/electronic plane ticket and boarding pass to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that Management require the immediate submission of the lacking documents to the audit team for verification and audit purposes in accordance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the management ensure that deduction of 10% retention money is made mandatory from the progress payment to the contractor to serve as warranty for hidden defects during the construction period and/or delivery of goods and shall only be released upon final acceptance of the project or upon request for substitution in lieu of the said retention money.

We recommended that Management ensure the submission of all the perfected contracts together with the complete supporting documents within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the auditor to conduct timely review and evaluation of the transactions.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere with the provisions of Sections 7.3.5, 7.4 and 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 by preparing and/or submitting to GPPB the final APP and semestral PMR within the stipulated deadline and using the prescribed format.

We recommended that the LDRRMO ensure strict compliance with the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and other DRRM-related rules and regulations particularly in the preparation of LDRRMFIP of the agency to establish proper management of the fund and the regularity of all charges therefrom.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, expedite the preparation of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance with RA 9003 and other pertinent regulations issued by the DENR and DILG to ensure the protection of the public from health and environment hazards.
Likewise, ensure to integrate the closure and rehabilitation plan of the existing open dump site for the Municipality to fully comply with the afore-cited regulations.
We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer require the personnel under the “job order” status to submit verified/accepted accomplishment report every month before paying their wages.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the amount appropriated for Gender and Development (GAD) to afford full implementation of the identified programs, activities, and projects (PAPs) in the GAD Plan so that the benefits that could be derived therefrom could be availed by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management stop granting the maximum allowable amount of Subsistence and Laundry Allowances to Municipal Health Workers and consider the provisions set forth under DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2012 in its computation of the said allowances. We also recommended the Management to require the officers concerned to refund the excessive allowances paid to them.

We recommended that monthly payments of honoraria to national government officials be stopped until the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality pass a resolution authorizing the grant thereof and specifying the rates of said allowances, subject to the availability of funds.

We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive require the submission of a report of actual service exposing the municipal health officials and employees to danger, perils to life and physical hardships in support to the payment of their hazard pay as determined by the Local Chief Executive in compliance with Section 3.1 of DBM and DOH Joint Circular.

We recommended that the Accounting Office, in cooperation with the Treasury Office, ensure the complete submission of accounts within the prescribed period for timely examination and verification of accounts pursuant to Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts, and Section 39 (1) and (3) of P.D 1445, thus to avoid disciplinary actions and/or penalties  provided for under Section 39 (3) of P.D 1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
